query: In our Stock context, the event model has an outbound node for ReserveCapacityOnOrderPlaced — it should emit a ReserveCapacity command to the Warehouse context whenever an OrderPlaced event fires. The Outbound spec is red. I need to create src/Store/Stock/Integrations/ReserveCapacityOnOrderPlaced.hs with the nullary marker type, the EntityOf instance, a total handleEvent, and close it with the TH macro. Walk me through the full file.

need a periodic timer in our cart context that fires a PurgeAbandonedCarts command every 10 minutes. where does the Integration.Timer value live and whats the exact syntax for Timer.every in neohaskell? should this go in Integrations.hs or a separate file

I have src/Library/Integrations/NotifyMemberOnBookReturned.hs that was scaffolded during domain modeling — handleEvent is just returning Integration.none with a TODO. The Outbound unit test is now red on the assertion. I need to fill in the real logic: when a BookReturned event fires, emit a SendMemberNotification command with the memberId and bookId from the entity and event. Show me what handleEvent should look like.

Our dispatcher keeps crashing on replay whenever any CartEvent comes through. I found the culprit: src/Store/Cart/Integrations/NotifyWarehouseOnItemAdded.hs has a `panic "TODO"` inside handleEvent. What is the safe way to stub this so it compiles and runs without crashing the dispatcher while we still have the unit test failing on the assertion?

The event model for our Library feature has an outbound kind node: when a LoanCreated event fires on a Member entity, we need to cross-aggregate dispatch a ReserveItem command into the Catalog context. I don't want to name the integration explicitly — just explain the full outbound per-trigger pattern: nullary data type, EntityOf type instance, pure handleEvent with the wildcard, and the closing macro.

I need a stateful lifecycle outbound integration for the Stock context that keeps a running adjustment log per StockEntity — it needs initialize to set up the initial state, processEvent to accumulate adjustments, and cleanup to flush. Looks like withOutboundLifecycle territory. How do I write the Lifecycle.OutboundConfig and what type does processEvent return exactly?

writing an inbound webhook integration for the Cart context — an external payment gateway will POST to trigger a ConfirmPayment command. i think this is Integration.Inbound not a timer. does it still go in Integrations.hs and how do i wire the toCommand lambda when theres no schedule involved

After implement-event-and-update-entity added ItemAddedToCart to our CartEvent ADT, the domain modeling phase stubbed out src/Store/Cart/Integrations/UpdateInventoryOnItemAdded.hs with Integration.none. The Outbound spec is now failing on the assertion — not a type error. I need to implement handleEvent so it emits a DecrementInventory command to the Stock context with stockId and quantity pulled from the ItemAddedToCart event fields and the CartEntity. What goes in the file?

I've finished writing the ReserveStockOnItemAdded integration and neo build passes. Now I need to register it in App.hs so the dispatcher actually picks it up — which application combinator do I use and what is the exact line to add to the pipeline for a per-trigger outbound handler?

The event model for our Stock context has a ReserveStock command coming in from the Cart context. I need to implement the command handler: the command type itself, the handleCommand function that validates available quantity and emits a StockReserved or InsufficientStock event, and any guard logic. Walk me through the NeoHaskell command implementation pattern.

After my unit test scaffold is red for the Cart context, I need to add the ItemAdded variant to the CartEvent ADT and update CartEntity — specifically the items list field. What does the updateEntity function look like for appending an item, and how do I pattern-match on the event ADT to extract stockId and quantity?

I have integration nodes in event-model.json for the Library context with kind: outbound on LoanCreated. Before I write any real logic I need to scaffold the stub files: the nullary marker types and Integration.none placeholders so the project compiles and I can start the TDD cycle. What does the domain modeling phase produce for integration stubs?

I need to write the Outbound unit test for ReserveStockOnItemAdded in the Stock context before implementing anything — the test should go red on the assertion, not a type error. How do I structure the OutboundSpec, what helpers do I use to simulate the event firing, and how do I assert on the emitted command payload?

We are designing the checkout feature for our Cart service and I need to figure out the event model: when the Cart context raises an OrderPlaced event, should the notification to the Stock context be an outbound integration node or an inbound one? And how do I represent the cross-aggregate command flow in event-model.json — what fields does an integration node need?

Can you walk me through the full outside-in TDD cycle for a new Cart feature in NeoHaskell from scratch — starting from writing the hurl e2e test, through feature tests, unit tests, domain modeling, filling in handlers, and finally wiring everything together? I want to understand all the steps before I start.

I need to add a reservedQuantity field to StockEntity so the ReserveStock command can track how much has been committed. What is the NeoHaskell pattern for adding a field to an existing entity record — do I need to update Core.hs, the event handler, and any snapshot serialization?
